* {'text': '兵庫県宍粟市一宮町森添280', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御形神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11489014}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Mikata Shrine |画像=[[file:御形神社 拝殿.jpg|280px]]Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}, Ichinomiya-chō Morizoe 280 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|21.9|N|134|37|47.9|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御形神社}} |祭神=Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}) |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} and {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御形神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mikata Shrine''' (みかたじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}} {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha||WD=|lt=minor Shikinaisha}}, and its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}), whose exploits are described in the "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}}." === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (nationally designated) === *Honden (main hall) :The only nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}} structure in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}.{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/147621 |title=文化遺産データベース「御形神社本殿」 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2015-12-06}} Built in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}, in Daiei 7 ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|WD=Q6412}}). Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. It is significant as an example of medieval shrine architecture whose construction date is clearly known. It was designated as a national Important Cultural Property on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|WD=Q2613}}, {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648}} (Shōwa 42). Dismantling and restoration repairs were carried out in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}--{{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}} (Shōwa 46--47), which revived the carvings and delicate bracket complexes from the time of the original late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} construction. === Shisō City designated cultural property === *Hyakunin Isshu illustrated picture votive tablets (ema) :There are 17 ema panels in total. Examples depicting all one hundred poets are rare nationwide, and these are the only such examples in the prefecture. They are in good condition and can be regarded as extremely valuable surviving artifacts. Two inscriptions of provenance are attached, from which it is known that they were dedicated in Kōka 3 ({{ill|lt=1846|Q7704|WD=Q7704}}). === Festivals === *Saitan-sai (January 1) *Setsubun-sai (day of Setsubun) *Kinen-sai (February 17) *Kaitō-sai (March 7) *Hōgei-sai (March 17) *Spring Festival (May 3) *Chūkon-sai (May 25) *Ōharae (June 30) *Fūnan-yoke Gandachi-sai (August 3) *Fūnan-yoke Kaigan-sai (August 29) *Autumn Festival (October 10) *Niiname-sai (November 23) *Ōharae and Joya-sai (December 31) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.mikata-jinja.com Mikata Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

* {'text': '兵庫県宍粟市一宮町森添280', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御形神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11489014}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Mikata Shrine |画像=[[file:御形神社 拝殿.jpg|280px]]Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}, Ichinomiya-chō Morizoe 280 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|21.9|N|134|37|47.9|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御形神社}} |祭神=Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}) |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} and {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御形神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mikata Shrine''' (みかたじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}} {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=minor Shikinaisha}}, and its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}), whose exploits are described in the "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}}." === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (nationally designated) === *Honden (main hall) :The only nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}} structure in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}.{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/147621 |title=文化遺産データベース「御形神社本殿」 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2015-12-06}} Built in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}, in Daiei 7 ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|WD=Q6412}}). Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. It is significant as an example of medieval shrine architecture whose construction date is clearly known. It was designated as a national Important Cultural Property on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|WD=Q2613}}, {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648}} (Shōwa 42). Dismantling and restoration repairs were carried out in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}--{{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}} (Shōwa 46--47), which revived the carvings and delicate bracket complexes from the time of the original late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} construction. === Shisō City designated cultural property === *Hyakunin Isshu illustrated picture votive tablets (ema) :There are 17 ema panels in total. Examples depicting all one hundred poets are rare nationwide, and these are the only such examples in the prefecture. They are in good condition and can be regarded as extremely valuable surviving artifacts. Two inscriptions of provenance are attached, from which it is known that they were dedicated in Kōka 3 ({{ill|lt=1846|Q7704|WD=Q7704}}). === Festivals === *Saitan-sai (January 1) *Setsubun-sai (day of Setsubun) *Kinen-sai (February 17) *Kaitō-sai (March 7) *Hōgei-sai (March 17) *Spring Festival (May 3) *Chūkon-sai (May 25) *Ōharae (June 30) *Fūnan-yoke Gandachi-sai (August 3) *Fūnan-yoke Kaigan-sai (August 29) *Autumn Festival (October 10) *Niiname-sai (November 23) *Ōharae and Joya-sai (December 31) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.mikata-jinja.com Mikata Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

* {'text': '兵庫県宍粟市一宮町森添280', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御形神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11489014}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Mikata Shrine |画像=[[file:御形神社 拝殿.jpg|280px]]Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}, Ichinomiya-chō Morizoe 280 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|21.9|N|134|37|47.9|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御形神社}} |祭神=Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}) |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} and {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御形神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mikata Shrine''' (みかたじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}} {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}}, and its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}), whose exploits are described in the "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}}." === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (nationally designated) === *Honden (main hall) :The only nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}} structure in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}.{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/147621 |title=文化遺産データベース「御形神社本殿」 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2015-12-06}} Built in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}, in Daiei 7 ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|WD=Q6412}}). Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. It is significant as an example of medieval shrine architecture whose construction date is clearly known. It was designated as a national Important Cultural Property on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|WD=Q2613}}, {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648}} (Shōwa 42). Dismantling and restoration repairs were carried out in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}--{{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}} (Shōwa 46--47), which revived the carvings and delicate bracket complexes from the time of the original late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} construction. === Shisō City designated cultural property === *Hyakunin Isshu illustrated picture votive tablets (ema) :There are 17 ema panels in total. Examples depicting all one hundred poets are rare nationwide, and these are the only such examples in the prefecture. They are in good condition and can be regarded as extremely valuable surviving artifacts. Two inscriptions of provenance are attached, from which it is known that they were dedicated in Kōka 3 ({{ill|lt=1846|Q7704|WD=Q7704}}). === Festivals === *Saitan-sai (January 1) *Setsubun-sai (day of Setsubun) *Kinen-sai (February 17) *Kaitō-sai (March 7) *Hōgei-sai (March 17) *Spring Festival (May 3) *Chūkon-sai (May 25) *Ōharae (June 30) *Fūnan-yoke Gandachi-sai (August 3) *Fūnan-yoke Kaigan-sai (August 29) *Autumn Festival (October 10) *Niiname-sai (November 23) *Ōharae and Joya-sai (December 31) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.mikata-jinja.com Mikata Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

* {'text': '兵庫県宍粟市一宮町森添280', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御形神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11489014}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Mikata Shrine |画像=[[file:御形神社 拝殿.jpg|280px]]Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}, Ichinomiya-chō Morizoe 280 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|21.9|N|134|37|47.9|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御形神社}} |祭神=Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}) |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} and {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御形神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mikata Shrine''' (みかたじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}} {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}}, and its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}), whose exploits are described in the "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}}." === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (nationally designated) === *Honden (main hall) :The only nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}} structure in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}.{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/147621 |title=文化遺産データベース「御形神社本殿」 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2015-12-06}} Built in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}, in Daiei 7 ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|WD=Q6412}}). Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. It is significant as an example of medieval shrine architecture whose construction date is clearly known. It was designated as a national Important Cultural Property on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|WD=Q2613}}, {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648}} (Shōwa 42). Dismantling and restoration repairs were carried out in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}--{{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}} (Shōwa 46--47), which revived the carvings and delicate bracket complexes from the time of the original late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} construction. === Shisō City designated cultural property === *Hyakunin Isshu illustrated picture votive tablets (ema) :There are 17 ema panels in total. Examples depicting all one hundred poets are rare nationwide, and these are the only such examples in the prefecture. They are in good condition and can be regarded as extremely valuable surviving artifacts. Two inscriptions of provenance are attached, from which it is known that they were dedicated in Kōka 3 ({{ill|lt=1846|Q7704|WD=Q7704}}). === Festivals === *Saitan-sai (January 1) *Setsubun-sai (day of Setsubun) *Kinen-sai (February 17) *Kaitō-sai (March 7) *Hōgei-sai (March 17) *Spring Festival (May 3) *Chūkon-sai (May 25) *Ōharae (June 30) *Fūnan-yoke Gandachi-sai (August 3) *Fūnan-yoke Kaigan-sai (August 29) *Autumn Festival (October 10) *Niiname-sai (November 23) *Ōharae and Joya-sai (December 31) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.mikata-jinja.com Mikata Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

* {'text': '兵庫県宍粟市一宮町森添280', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御形神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11489014}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Mikata Shrine |画像=[[file:御形神社 拝殿.jpg|280px]]Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}, Ichinomiya-chō Morizoe 280 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|21.9|N|134|37|47.9|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御形神社}} |祭神=Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}) |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} and {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御形神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mikata Shrine''' (みかたじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}} {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}}, and its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}), whose exploits are described in the "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}}." === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (nationally designated) === *Honden (main hall) :The only nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}} structure in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}.{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/147621 |title=文化遺産データベース「御形神社本殿」 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2015-12-06}} Built in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}, in Daiei 7 ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|WD=Q6412}}). Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. It is significant as an example of medieval shrine architecture whose construction date is clearly known. It was designated as a national Important Cultural Property on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|WD=Q2613}}, {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648}} (Shōwa 42). Dismantling and restoration repairs were carried out in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}--{{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}} (Shōwa 46--47), which revived the carvings and delicate bracket complexes from the time of the original late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} construction. === Shisō City designated cultural property === *Hyakunin Isshu illustrated picture votive tablets (ema) :There are 17 ema panels in total. Examples depicting all one hundred poets are rare nationwide, and these are the only such examples in the prefecture. They are in good condition and can be regarded as extremely valuable surviving artifacts. Two inscriptions of provenance are attached, from which it is known that they were dedicated in Kōka 3 ({{ill|lt=1846|Q7704|WD=Q7704}}). === Festivals === *Saitan-sai (January 1) *Setsubun-sai (day of Setsubun) *Kinen-sai (February 17) *Kaitō-sai (March 7) *Hōgei-sai (March 17) *Spring Festival (May 3) *Chūkon-sai (May 25) *Ōharae (June 30) *Fūnan-yoke Gandachi-sai (August 3) *Fūnan-yoke Kaigan-sai (August 29) *Autumn Festival (October 10) *Niiname-sai (November 23) *Ōharae and Joya-sai (December 31) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.mikata-jinja.com Mikata Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

* {'text': '兵庫県宍粟市一宮町森添280', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御形神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11489014}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Mikata Shrine |画像=[[file:御形神社 拝殿.jpg|280px]]Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}, Ichinomiya-chō Morizoe 280 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|21.9|N|134|37|47.9|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御形神社}} |祭神=Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}) |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} and {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御形神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mikata Shrine''' (みかたじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}} {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}}, and its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}}), whose exploits are described in the "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}}." === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (nationally designated) === *Honden (main hall) :The only nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}} structure in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216}}.{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/147621 |title=文化遺産データベース「御形神社本殿」 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2015-12-06}} Built in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}, in Daiei 7 ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|WD=Q6412}}). Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. It is significant as an example of medieval shrine architecture whose construction date is clearly known. It was designated as a national Important Cultural Property on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|WD=Q2613}}, {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648}} (Shōwa 42). Dismantling and restoration repairs were carried out in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}--{{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}} (Shōwa 46--47), which revived the carvings and delicate bracket complexes from the time of the original late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} construction. === Shisō City designated cultural property === *Hyakunin Isshu illustrated picture votive tablets (ema) :There are 17 ema panels in total. Examples depicting all one hundred poets are rare nationwide, and these are the only such examples in the prefecture. They are in good condition and can be regarded as extremely valuable surviving artifacts. Two inscriptions of provenance are attached, from which it is known that they were dedicated in Kōka 3 ({{ill|lt=1846|Q7704|WD=Q7704}}). === Festivals === *Saitan-sai (January 1) *Setsubun-sai (day of Setsubun) *Kinen-sai (February 17) *Kaitō-sai (March 7) *Hōgei-sai (March 17) *Spring Festival (May 3) *Chūkon-sai (May 25) *Ōharae (June 30) *Fūnan-yoke Gandachi-sai (August 3) *Fūnan-yoke Kaigan-sai (August 29) *Autumn Festival (October 10) *Niiname-sai (November 23) *Ōharae and Joya-sai (December 31) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.mikata-jinja.com Mikata Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * みかたじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 259370082 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7140005007160 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00633666 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宍粟市一宮町森添280', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御形神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11489014}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Mikata Shrine |画像=[[file:御形神社 拝殿.jpg|280px]]Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}, Ichinomiya-chō Morizoe 280 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|21.9|N|134|37|47.9|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御形神社}} |祭神=Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} and {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御形神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mikata Shrine''' (みかたじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}}, and its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}), whose exploits are described in the "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}." === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (nationally designated) === *Honden (main hall) :The only nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} structure in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}.{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/147621 |title=文化遺産データベース「御形神社本殿」 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2015-12-06}} Built in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, in Daiei 7 ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|WD=Q6412|1=1527}}). Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. It is significant as an example of medieval shrine architecture whose construction date is clearly known. It was designated as a national Important Cultural Property on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|WD=Q2613|1=June 15}}, {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42). Dismantling and restoration repairs were carried out in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}--{{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}} (Shōwa 46--47), which revived the carvings and delicate bracket complexes from the time of the original late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} construction. === Shisō City designated cultural property === *Hyakunin Isshu illustrated picture votive tablets (ema) :There are 17 ema panels in total. Examples depicting all one hundred poets are rare nationwide, and these are the only such examples in the prefecture. They are in good condition and can be regarded as extremely valuable surviving artifacts. Two inscriptions of provenance are attached, from which it is known that they were dedicated in Kōka 3 ({{ill|lt=1846|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}}). === Festivals === *Saitan-sai (January 1) *Setsubun-sai (day of Setsubun) *Kinen-sai (February 17) *Kaitō-sai (March 7) *Hōgei-sai (March 17) *Spring Festival (May 3) *Chūkon-sai (May 25) *Ōharae (June 30) *Fūnan-yoke Gandachi-sai (August 3) *Fūnan-yoke Kaigan-sai (August 29) *Autumn Festival (October 10) *Niiname-sai (November 23) *Ōharae and Joya-sai (December 31) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.mikata-jinja.com Mikata Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== * {'text': '兵庫県宍粟市一宮町森添280', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御形神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11489014}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Mikata Shrine |画像=[[file:御形神社 拝殿.jpg|280px]]Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}, Ichinomiya-chō Morizoe 280 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|21.9|N|134|37|47.9|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御形神社}} |祭神=Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} and {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御形神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mikata Shrine''' (みかたじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}}, and its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}), whose exploits are described in the "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}." === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (nationally designated) === *Honden (main hall) :The only nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} structure in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}.{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/147621 |title=文化遺産データベース「御形神社本殿」 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2015-12-06}} Built in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, in Daiei 7 ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|WD=Q6412|1=1527}}). Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. It is significant as an example of medieval shrine architecture whose construction date is clearly known. It was designated as a national Important Cultural Property on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|WD=Q2613|1=June 15}}, {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42). Dismantling and restoration repairs were carried out in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}--{{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}} (Shōwa 46--47), which revived the carvings and delicate bracket complexes from the time of the original late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} construction. === Shisō City designated cultural property === *Hyakunin Isshu illustrated picture votive tablets (ema) :There are 17 ema panels in total. Examples depicting all one hundred poets are rare nationwide, and these are the only such examples in the prefecture. They are in good condition and can be regarded as extremely valuable surviving artifacts. Two inscriptions of provenance are attached, from which it is known that they were dedicated in Kōka 3 ({{ill|lt=1846|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}}). === Festivals === *Saitan-sai (January 1) *Setsubun-sai (day of Setsubun) *Kinen-sai (February 17) *Kaitō-sai (March 7) *Hōgei-sai (March 17) *Spring Festival (May 3) *Chūkon-sai (May 25) *Ōharae (June 30) *Fūnan-yoke Gandachi-sai (August 3) *Fūnan-yoke Kaigan-sai (August 29) *Autumn Festival (October 10) *Niiname-sai (November 23) *Ōharae and Joya-sai (December 31) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.mikata-jinja.com Mikata Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * みかたじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 259370082 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7140005007160 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00633666 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宍粟市一宮町森添280', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御形神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11489014}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Mikata Shrine |画像=[[file:御形神社 拝殿.jpg|280px]]Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}, Ichinomiya-chō Morizoe 280 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|21.9|N|134|37|47.9|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御形神社}} |祭神=Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} and {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御形神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mikata Shrine''' (みかたじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}}, and its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}), whose exploits are described in the "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}." === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (nationally designated) === *Honden (main hall) :The only nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} structure in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}.{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/147621 |title=文化遺産データベース「御形神社本殿」 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2015-12-06}} Built in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, in Daiei 7 ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|WD=Q6412|1=1527}}). Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. It is significant as an example of medieval shrine architecture whose construction date is clearly known. It was designated as a national Important Cultural Property on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|WD=Q2613|1=June 15}}, {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42). Dismantling and restoration repairs were carried out in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}--{{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}} (Shōwa 46--47), which revived the carvings and delicate bracket complexes from the time of the original late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} construction. === Shisō City designated cultural property === *Hyakunin Isshu illustrated picture votive tablets (ema) :There are 17 ema panels in total. Examples depicting all one hundred poets are rare nationwide, and these are the only such examples in the prefecture. They are in good condition and can be regarded as extremely valuable surviving artifacts. Two inscriptions of provenance are attached, from which it is known that they were dedicated in Kōka 3 ({{ill|lt=1846|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}}). === Festivals === *Saitan-sai (January 1) *Setsubun-sai (day of Setsubun) *Kinen-sai (February 17) *Kaitō-sai (March 7) *Hōgei-sai (March 17) *Spring Festival (May 3) *Chūkon-sai (May 25) *Ōharae (June 30) *Fūnan-yoke Gandachi-sai (August 3) *Fūnan-yoke Kaigan-sai (August 29) *Autumn Festival (October 10) *Niiname-sai (November 23) *Ōharae and Joya-sai (December 31) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.mikata-jinja.com Mikata Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みかたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * みかたじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 259370082 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7140005007160 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00633666 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宍粟市一宮町森添280', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御形神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11489014}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Mikata Shrine |画像=[[file:御形神社 拝殿.jpg|280px]]Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}, Ichinomiya-chō Morizoe 280 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|21.9|N|134|37|47.9|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御形神社}} |祭神=Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} and {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御形神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mikata Shrine''' (みかたじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}}, and its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}), whose exploits are described in the "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}." === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (nationally designated) === *Honden (main hall) :The only nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} structure in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}.{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/147621 |title=文化遺産データベース「御形神社本殿」 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2015-12-06}} Built in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, in Daiei 7 ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|WD=Q6412|1=1527}}). Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. It is significant as an example of medieval shrine architecture whose construction date is clearly known. It was designated as a national Important Cultural Property on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|WD=Q2613|1=June 15}}, {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42). Dismantling and restoration repairs were carried out in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}--{{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}} (Shōwa 46--47), which revived the carvings and delicate bracket complexes from the time of the original late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} construction. === Shisō City designated cultural property === *Hyakunin Isshu illustrated picture votive tablets (ema) :There are 17 ema panels in total. Examples depicting all one hundred poets are rare nationwide, and these are the only such examples in the prefecture. They are in good condition and can be regarded as extremely valuable surviving artifacts. Two inscriptions of provenance are attached, from which it is known that they were dedicated in Kōka 3 ({{ill|lt=1846|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}}). === Festivals === *Saitan-sai (January 1) *Setsubun-sai (day of Setsubun) *Kinen-sai (February 17) *Kaitō-sai (March 7) *Hōgei-sai (March 17) *Spring Festival (May 3) *Chūkon-sai (May 25) *Ōharae (June 30) *Fūnan-yoke Gandachi-sai (August 3) *Fūnan-yoke Kaigan-sai (August 29) *Autumn Festival (October 10) *Niiname-sai (November 23) *Ōharae and Joya-sai (December 31) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.mikata-jinja.com Mikata Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * みかたじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 259370082 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7140005007160 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00633666 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宍粟市一宮町森添280', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御形神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11489014}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Mikata Shrine |画像=[[file:御形神社 拝殿.jpg|280px]]Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}, Ichinomiya-chō Morizoe 280 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|21.9|N|134|37|47.9|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御形神社}} |祭神=Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} and {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御形神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mikata Shrine''' (みかたじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}}, and its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}), whose exploits are described in the "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}." === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (nationally designated) === *Honden (main hall) :The only nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} structure in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}.{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/147621 |title=文化遺産データベース「御形神社本殿」 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2015-12-06}} Built in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, in Daiei 7 ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|WD=Q6412|1=1527}}). Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. It is significant as an example of medieval shrine architecture whose construction date is clearly known. It was designated as a national Important Cultural Property on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|WD=Q2613|1=June 15}}, {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42). Dismantling and restoration repairs were carried out in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}--{{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}} (Shōwa 46--47), which revived the carvings and delicate bracket complexes from the time of the original late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} construction. === Shisō City designated cultural property === *Hyakunin Isshu illustrated picture votive tablets (ema) :There are 17 ema panels in total. Examples depicting all one hundred poets are rare nationwide, and these are the only such examples in the prefecture. They are in good condition and can be regarded as extremely valuable surviving artifacts. Two inscriptions of provenance are attached, from which it is known that they were dedicated in Kōka 3 ({{ill|lt=1846|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}}). === Festivals === *Saitan-sai (January 1) *Setsubun-sai (day of Setsubun) *Kinen-sai (February 17) *Kaitō-sai (March 7) *Hōgei-sai (March 17) *Spring Festival (May 3) *Chūkon-sai (May 25) *Ōharae (June 30) *Fūnan-yoke Gandachi-sai (August 3) *Fūnan-yoke Kaigan-sai (August 29) *Autumn Festival (October 10) *Niiname-sai (November 23) *Ōharae and Joya-sai (December 31) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.mikata-jinja.com Mikata Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:兵庫県の重要文化財]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * みかたじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 259370082 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7140005007160 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00633666 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宍粟市一宮町森添280', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御形神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11489014}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Mikata Shrine |画像=[[file:御形神社 拝殿.jpg|280px]]Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}, Ichinomiya-chō Morizoe 280 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|21.9|N|134|37|47.9|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御形神社}} |祭神=Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} and {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御形神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mikata Shrine''' (みかたじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}}, and its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}), whose exploits are described in the "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}." === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (nationally designated) === *Honden (main hall) :The only nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} structure in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}.{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/147621 |title=文化遺産データベース「御形神社本殿」 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2015-12-06}} Built in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, in Daiei 7 ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|WD=Q6412|1=1527}}). Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. It is significant as an example of medieval shrine architecture whose construction date is clearly known. It was designated as a national Important Cultural Property on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|WD=Q2613|1=June 15}}, {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42). Dismantling and restoration repairs were carried out in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}--{{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}} (Shōwa 46--47), which revived the carvings and delicate bracket complexes from the time of the original late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} construction. === Shisō City designated cultural property === *Hyakunin Isshu illustrated picture votive tablets (ema) :There are 17 ema panels in total. Examples depicting all one hundred poets are rare nationwide, and these are the only such examples in the prefecture. They are in good condition and can be regarded as extremely valuable surviving artifacts. Two inscriptions of provenance are attached, from which it is known that they were dedicated in Kōka 3 ({{ill|lt=1846|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}}). === Festivals === *Saitan-sai (January 1) *Setsubun-sai (day of Setsubun) *Kinen-sai (February 17) *Kaitō-sai (March 7) *Hōgei-sai (March 17) *Spring Festival (May 3) *Chūkon-sai (May 25) *Ōharae (June 30) *Fūnan-yoke Gandachi-sai (August 3) *Fūnan-yoke Kaigan-sai (August 29) *Autumn Festival (October 10) *Niiname-sai (November 23) *Ōharae and Joya-sai (December 31) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.mikata-jinja.com Mikata Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]][[Category:qq]]

== * {'text': '兵庫県宍粟市一宮町森添280', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御形神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11489014}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Mikata Shrine |画像=[[file:御形神社 拝殿.jpg|280px]]Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}, Ichinomiya-chō Morizoe 280 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|21.9|N|134|37|47.9|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御形神社}} |祭神=Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} and {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御形神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mikata Shrine''' (みかたじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}}, and its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}), whose exploits are described in the "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}." === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (nationally designated) === *Honden (main hall) :The only nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} structure in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}.{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/147621 |title=文化遺産データベース「御形神社本殿」 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2015-12-06}} Built in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, in Daiei 7 ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|WD=Q6412|1=1527}}). Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. It is significant as an example of medieval shrine architecture whose construction date is clearly known. It was designated as a national Important Cultural Property on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|WD=Q2613|1=June 15}}, {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42). Dismantling and restoration repairs were carried out in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}--{{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}} (Shōwa 46--47), which revived the carvings and delicate bracket complexes from the time of the original late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} construction. === Shisō City designated cultural property === *Hyakunin Isshu illustrated picture votive tablets (ema) :There are 17 ema panels in total. Examples depicting all one hundred poets are rare nationwide, and these are the only such examples in the prefecture. They are in good condition and can be regarded as extremely valuable surviving artifacts. Two inscriptions of provenance are attached, from which it is known that they were dedicated in Kōka 3 ({{ill|lt=1846|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}}). === Festivals === *Saitan-sai (January 1) *Setsubun-sai (day of Setsubun) *Kinen-sai (February 17) *Kaitō-sai (March 7) *Hōgei-sai (March 17) *Spring Festival (May 3) *Chūkon-sai (May 25) *Ōharae (June 30) *Fūnan-yoke Gandachi-sai (August 3) *Fūnan-yoke Kaigan-sai (August 29) *Autumn Festival (October 10) *Niiname-sai (November 23) *Ōharae and Joya-sai (December 31) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.mikata-jinja.com Mikata Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]][[Category:qq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * みかたじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 259370082 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7140005007160 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00633666 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宍粟市一宮町森添280', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御形神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11489014}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Mikata Shrine |画像=[[file:御形神社 拝殿.jpg|280px]]Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}, Ichinomiya-chō Morizoe 280 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|21.9|N|134|37|47.9|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御形神社}} |祭神=Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} and {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御形神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mikata Shrine''' (みかたじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}}, and its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}), whose exploits are described in the "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}." === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (nationally designated) === *Honden (main hall) :The only nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} structure in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}.{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/147621 |title=文化遺産データベース「御形神社本殿」 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2015-12-06}} Built in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, in Daiei 7 ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|WD=Q6412|1=1527}}). Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. It is significant as an example of medieval shrine architecture whose construction date is clearly known. It was designated as a national Important Cultural Property on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|WD=Q2613|1=June 15}}, {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42). Dismantling and restoration repairs were carried out in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}--{{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}} (Shōwa 46--47), which revived the carvings and delicate bracket complexes from the time of the original late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} construction. === Shisō City designated cultural property === *Hyakunin Isshu illustrated picture votive tablets (ema) :There are 17 ema panels in total. Examples depicting all one hundred poets are rare nationwide, and these are the only such examples in the prefecture. They are in good condition and can be regarded as extremely valuable surviving artifacts. Two inscriptions of provenance are attached, from which it is known that they were dedicated in Kōka 3 ({{ill|lt=1846|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}}). === Festivals === *Saitan-sai (January 1) *Setsubun-sai (day of Setsubun) *Kinen-sai (February 17) *Kaitō-sai (March 7) *Hōgei-sai (March 17) *Spring Festival (May 3) *Chūkon-sai (May 25) *Ōharae (June 30) *Fūnan-yoke Gandachi-sai (August 3) *Fūnan-yoke Kaigan-sai (August 29) *Autumn Festival (October 10) *Niiname-sai (November 23) *Ōharae and Joya-sai (December 31) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.mikata-jinja.com Mikata Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]][[Category:qq]]

== * {'text': '兵庫県宍粟市一宮町森添280', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御形神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11489014}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Mikata Shrine |画像=[[file:御形神社 拝殿.jpg|280px]]Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}, Ichinomiya-chō Morizoe 280 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|21.9|N|134|37|47.9|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御形神社}} |祭神=Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} and {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御形神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mikata Shrine''' (みかたじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}}, and its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}), whose exploits are described in the "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}." === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (nationally designated) === *Honden (main hall) :The only nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} structure in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}.{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/147621 |title=文化遺産データベース「御形神社本殿」 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2015-12-06}} Built in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, in Daiei 7 ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|WD=Q6412|1=1527}}). Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. It is significant as an example of medieval shrine architecture whose construction date is clearly known. It was designated as a national Important Cultural Property on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|WD=Q2613|1=June 15}}, {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42). Dismantling and restoration repairs were carried out in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}--{{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}} (Shōwa 46--47), which revived the carvings and delicate bracket complexes from the time of the original late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} construction. === Shisō City designated cultural property === *Hyakunin Isshu illustrated picture votive tablets (ema) :There are 17 ema panels in total. Examples depicting all one hundred poets are rare nationwide, and these are the only such examples in the prefecture. They are in good condition and can be regarded as extremely valuable surviving artifacts. Two inscriptions of provenance are attached, from which it is known that they were dedicated in Kōka 3 ({{ill|lt=1846|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}}). === Festivals === *Saitan-sai (January 1) *Setsubun-sai (day of Setsubun) *Kinen-sai (February 17) *Kaitō-sai (March 7) *Hōgei-sai (March 17) *Spring Festival (May 3) *Chūkon-sai (May 25) *Ōharae (June 30) *Fūnan-yoke Gandachi-sai (August 3) *Fūnan-yoke Kaigan-sai (August 29) *Autumn Festival (October 10) *Niiname-sai (November 23) *Ōharae and Joya-sai (December 31) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.mikata-jinja.com Mikata Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * みかたじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 259370082 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7140005007160 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00633666 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宍粟市一宮町森添280', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御形神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11489014}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Mikata Shrine |画像=[[file:御形神社 拝殿.jpg|280px]]Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}, Ichinomiya-chō Morizoe 280 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|21.9|N|134|37|47.9|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御形神社}} |祭神=Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|WD=Q30729|1=772}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} and {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御形神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mikata Shrine''' (みかたじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}}, and its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}), whose exploits are described in the "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}." === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (nationally designated) === *Honden (main hall) :The only nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} structure in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|WD=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}.{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/147621 |title=文化遺産データベース「御形神社本殿」 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2015-12-06}} Built in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, in Daiei 7 ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|WD=Q6412|1=1527}}). Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. It is significant as an example of medieval shrine architecture whose construction date is clearly known. It was designated as a national Important Cultural Property on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|WD=Q2613|1=June 15}}, {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42). Dismantling and restoration repairs were carried out in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475|1=1971}}--{{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476|1=1972}} (Shōwa 46--47), which revived the carvings and delicate bracket complexes from the time of the original late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} construction. === Shisō City designated cultural property === *Hyakunin Isshu illustrated picture votive tablets (ema) :There are 17 ema panels in total. Examples depicting all one hundred poets are rare nationwide, and these are the only such examples in the prefecture. They are in good condition and can be regarded as extremely valuable surviving artifacts. Two inscriptions of provenance are attached, from which it is known that they were dedicated in Kōka 3 ({{ill|lt=1846|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}}). === Festivals === *Saitan-sai (January 1) *Setsubun-sai (day of Setsubun) *Kinen-sai (February 17) *Kaitō-sai (March 7) *Hōgei-sai (March 17) *Spring Festival (May 3) *Chūkon-sai (May 25) *Ōharae (June 30) *Fūnan-yoke Gandachi-sai (August 3) *Fūnan-yoke Kaigan-sai (August 29) *Autumn Festival (October 10) *Niiname-sai (November 23) *Ōharae and Joya-sai (December 31) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.mikata-jinja.com Mikata Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * みかたじんじゃ == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 259370082 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7140005007160 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00633666 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県宍粟市一宮町森添280', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御形神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11489014}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Mikata Shrine |画像=[[file:御形神社 拝殿.jpg|280px]]Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|qid=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}, Ichinomiya-chō Morizoe 280 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|11|21.9|N|134|37|47.9|E|scale:10000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=御形神社}} |祭神=Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|qid=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 3 ({{ill|lt=772|Q30729|qid=Q30729|1=772}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|qid=Q2547|1=May 3}} and {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|qid=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=御形神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Mikata Shrine''' (みかたじんじゃ) is a shrine located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|qid=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}}, and its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was prefectural shrine. It enshrines Ashiharashikoo no Kami ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}), whose exploits are described in the "{{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|qid=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}}." === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (nationally designated) === *Honden (main hall) :The only nationally designated {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|qid=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} structure in {{ill|lt=Shisō|Q877216|qid=Q877216|1=Shisō, Hyōgo}}.{{Cite web|和書|author= |year= |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/147621 |title=文化遺産データベース「御形神社本殿」 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2015-12-06}} Built in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, in Daiei 7 ({{ill|lt=1527|Q6412|qid=Q6412|1=1527}}). Three-bay nagare-zukuri style with cypress bark roofing. It is significant as an example of medieval shrine architecture whose construction date is clearly known. It was designated as a national Important Cultural Property on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|qid=Q2613|1=June 15}}, {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|qid=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42). Dismantling and restoration repairs were carried out in {{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|qid=Q2475|1=1971}}--{{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|qid=Q2476|1=1972}} (Shōwa 46--47), which revived the carvings and delicate bracket complexes from the time of the original late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} construction. === Shisō City designated cultural property === *Hyakunin Isshu illustrated picture votive tablets (ema) :There are 17 ema panels in total. Examples depicting all one hundred poets are rare nationwide, and these are the only such examples in the prefecture. They are in good condition and can be regarded as extremely valuable surviving artifacts. Two inscriptions of provenance are attached, from which it is known that they were dedicated in Kōka 3 ({{ill|lt=1846|Q7704|qid=Q7704|1=1864}}). === Festivals === *Saitan-sai (January 1) *Setsubun-sai (day of Setsubun) *Kinen-sai (February 17) *Kaitō-sai (March 7) *Hōgei-sai (March 17) *Spring Festival (May 3) *Chūkon-sai (May 25) *Ōharae (June 30) *Fūnan-yoke Gandachi-sai (August 3) *Fūnan-yoke Kaigan-sai (August 29) *Autumn Festival (October 10) *Niiname-sai (November 23) *Ōharae and Joya-sai (December 31) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.mikata-jinja.com Mikata Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{pref-stub|pref=兵庫県}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mikata Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:宍粟市の建築物]] [[Category:宍粟市の歴史]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]]
